summary: react-simple-utils-kit postinstall exfiltrates Coze API tokens and abuses them against api.coze.cn
details: Package advertises itself as a React date-formatting utility (index.js ships a benign date-format helper as cover) but its package.json declares a postinstall hook that runs postinstall.js on every npm install. postinstall.js reads the installer's COZE_WORKLOAD_API_TOKEN, COZE_PROJECT_SPACE_ID, and COZE_PROJECT_ID environment variables and POSTs them over plaintext HTTP to http://2e3bkumw.requestrepo.com (a Burp-Collaborator-style sink controlled by the attacker), including a token preview that enables later replay. Using the captured token, the same script then issues authenticated POST/PATCH/DELETE calls against api.coze.cn to create bots, rename workspaces, and delete workspace members — destructive write-operation abuse of the installer's own SaaS account, which the script header explicitly labels 'Coze API 写操作越权' (Coze API write-operation privilege escalation). The benign main module, empty author field, and unrelated stated purpose are decoy cover for the postinstall payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
